perturbation.original_text: A. This Agreement and the provisions hereof, except as otherwise provided, shall be in full force and effect commencing on the date of execution by both Parties and shall extend for an initial term of two (2) years. This Agreement shall be automatically renewed for additional extended terms each of two (2) years duration unless either party notifies the other in writing of its intention not to renew the Agreement, such notification to be provided at least ninety (90) days prior to the expiration of the then in-effect term. The initial two (2) year term as it might be extended herein is referred to herein as the "Term."
perturbation.changed_text: A. This Agreement and the provisions hereof, except as otherwise provided, shall be in full force and effect commencing on the date of execution by both Parties and shall extend for an initial term of two (2) years. This Agreement shall not be automatically renewed and will expire after the initial term of two years, unless both parties agree to renew in writing 30 days prior to the expiration of the initial term. The initial two (2) year term as it might be extended herein is referred to herein as the "Term."
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 8A states the agreement automatically renews unless a party gives written notice of non-renewal 90 days prior. The modified text states that the agreement will not automatically renew and requires a written agreement from both parties for renewal 30 days prior. This creates a contradiction regarding the renewal terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. This Agreement and the provisions hereof, except as otherwise provided, shall be in full force and effect commencing on the date of execution by both Parties and shall extend for an initial term of two (2) years. This Agreement shall not be automatically renewed and will expire after the initial term of two years, unless both parties agree to renew in writing 30 days prior to the expiration of the initial term. The initial two (2) year term as it might be extended herein is referred to herein as the "Term."
perturbation.justification: The original text stated the agreement automatically renews unless a party gives written notice of non-renewal 90 days prior. The changed text states that the agreement will not automatically renew and requires a written agreement from both parties for renewal 30 days prior. Since the contradicted text is the same as the changed text, there is a direct contradiction between the original text and the contradicted text, because the original text stated it would auto-renew and the changed/contradicted text states it will not auto-renew.

perturbation.original_text: A. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other in the event of a breach of a material provision hereof unless, during that thirty (30) day period, the party receiving the notice cures the breach. B. COMPANY may, in its unfettered discretion, terminate this Agreement at any time after first givingCONTENT PROVIDER ten (10) days advance notice thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: A. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other in the event of a breach of a material provision hereof unless, during that thirty (30) day period, the party receiving the notice cures the breach. B. COMPANY may terminate this Agreement only in the event that the CONTENT PROVIDER breaches this agreement.
perturbation.explanation: Section 9A originally allows either party to terminate the agreement for a material breach with 30 days' notice. Section 9B originally granted the Company the right to terminate for any reason with 10 days' notice. The change removes Company's 'unfettered discretion' and specifies that the Company can only terminate if the Content Provider breaches the agreement. This creates a contradiction as the Company's rights to terminate the agreement are reduced.
perturbation.contradicted_text: A. This Agreement may be terminated by either party upon thirty (30) days written notice to the other in the event of a breach of a material provision hereof unless, during that thirty (30) day period, the party receiving the notice cures the breach. B. COMPANY may terminate this Agreement only in the event that the CONTENT PROVIDER breaches this agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change restricts the Company's termination rights, now allowing termination only if the Content Provider breaches the agreement, which directly contradicts the original provision granting 'unfettered discretion' to terminate with 10 days' notice. While the Contradicted Text and Changed Text are the same here, the question is whether the change made to Section 9B creates a contradiction to the text in Section 9A. It does, because initially, Section 9B allowed for termination at any time, but now termination is only allowed if Content Provider breaches the agreement.
